summary: Ukrainian assault units reported liberating several villages in the Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, while Russian tactical aviation intensified guided‑bomb strikes across multiple front‑line oblasts. UAV activity remains high with numerous sightings over Chernihiv, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, and missile attacks hit Kremenchuk and Bila Tserkva. International support continues, highlighted by Finland's €128 million aid package.
key_points: Ukrainian assault troops captured Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk, expanding liberated territory. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524)

Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Donetsk regions, increasing strike pressure on front‑line areas. (Source: @kpszsu/63303; @kpszsu/63267)

Multiple UAVs were observed: a UAV on the south‑east of Zaporizhzhia heading toward Zaporizhzhia, and several reactive UAVs over Chernihiv moving westward toward Kyiv. (Source: @kpszsu/63317; @kpszsu/63288)
Missile attacks struck Kremenchuk (1‑2 rockets) and Bila Tserkva (multiple rockets), indicating continued Russian long‑range strike activity. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265; @mon1tor_ua/67271)
Russian air defence reported shooting down 127 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory in a single night. (Source: @tass_agency/378193)
Finland announced its 33rd military aid package to Ukraine valued at approximately €128 million, adding to prior deliveries of €3.4 billion. (Source: @UkraineNow/81542)
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were issued for south‑east, east and north‑east directions, highlighting heightened aerial threat to near‑front regions. (Source: @kpszsu/63295)

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian assault units captured Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk, expanding liberated territory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524). Russian tactical aviation intensified guided‑bomb strikes across Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Donetsk, raising the risk of civilian and military casualties (Source: @kpszsu/63303; @kpszsu/63267). UAV sightings and missile attacks continued, with UAVs observed over Chernihiv and Zaporizhzhia and rockets hitting Kremenchuk and Bila Tserkva (Source: @kpszsu/63317; @mon1tor_ua/67265).

points: Ukrainian assault troops of the "North" Guards Motor Rifle Division advanced up to 100 m in the Bachevsk area, captured two enemy soldiers from the 104th Motor Rifle Brigade, and reported progress of up to 500 m in twelve sectors on the Sumy front, with ongoing firefights in Ivolzhansk and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).

Ukrainian forces liberated the villages of Budarki and Karaichnoye in the Kharkiv region and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in the Dnipropetrovsk region within a 24‑hour period, according to the Ministry of Defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
Ukrainian special‑operations units received the honorary title "Heroes of UPA," prompting diplomatic criticism but indicating continued internal reorganization (Source: @UkraineNow/81532).
Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down multiple UAVs, though Russian claims state 127 Ukrainian UAVs were downed over Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/378193).

Russian tactical aviation conducted guided‑bomb releases on Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts, signalling a broadening of aerial strike zones (Source: @kpszsu/63303; @kpszsu/63267).
Russian air‑defence forces reported a group strike on Ukrainian military aerodromes and critical infrastructure, as announced by the Russian Ministry of Defence (Source: @tass_agency/378209).
Two Tu‑95MS bombers were relocated from Olyenya airfield to Engels airfield, indicating strategic bomber repositioning (Source: @eRadarrua/94268).

Russian authorities warned of possible attacks on military transport aircraft AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A targeting Kursk and Shaykovka airfields, suggesting preparation for larger weapon deliveries (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
Ukrainian assault units reported advances of up to 500 m in twelve sectors on the Sumy front, with ongoing firefights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka and surrounding areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk regions, increasing pressure on Ukrainian defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/63303).
UAV activity was noted over Sumy and neighboring oblasts, with several UAVs remaining in the airspace and expectations of new groups entering (Source: @eRadarrua/94229).
Russian forces claimed liberation of villages in the Dnipropetrovsk region, including Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye, as part of their east‑front operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region moving westward toward Kyiv, including reactive UAVs along the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/63290; @kpszsu/63288).
A UAV was reported heading from north‑east Chernihiv toward Snovsk and Semenivka, indicating possible reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/63325).
UAV sightings included a guided UAV near Horodny and another near Semenivka in Chernihiv oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/94286).
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings highlighted threats on north‑east directions, affecting northern front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63295).

Ukrainian forces reported successes on the Oleksandrivsky sector and liberated territory near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk, as well as clearing operations near Voronno, Sichneve, Piddubne, Tvystyi, Novokhatky and Zelenyi Hai (Source: @UkraineNow/81535).
UAV activity was noted on the south‑east of Zaporizhzhia heading toward Zaporizhzhia city (Source: @kpszsu/63317).
Russian tactical aviation warned of increased activity on south‑east, east and north‑east directions, affecting southern front regions (Source: @kpszsu/63295).

Missile attacks struck Kremenchuk (1‑2 rockets) and Bila Tserkva (multiple rockets), indicating continued Russian long‑range strike capability in the south‑central zone (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265; @mon1tor_ua/67271).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on multiple front‑line oblasts, including Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/63303; @kpszsu/63267).
Russian air‑defence reported downing 127 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory in one night (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Ukrainian air‑defence units reported shooting down drones in Tula (6), Voronezh (13) and ongoing operations over Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Two Tu‑142 aircraft and an Iskander missile system were reportedly destroyed by Ukrainian SBS forces at a military airfield near Taganrog on 30 May (Source: @UkraineNow/81550).

key_developments: Ukrainian assault troops liberated Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk, expanding front‑line gains. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Donetsk, intensifying aerial strikes. (Source: @kpszsu/63303; @kpszsu/63267)

Multiple UAVs were observed over Chernihiv moving westward toward Kyiv and over Zaporizhzhia heading toward the city, indicating heightened reconnaissance activity. (Source: @kpszsu/63317; @kpszsu/63288)
Missile attacks struck Kremenchuk (1‑2 rockets) and Bila Tserkva (multiple rockets), showing continued Russian long‑range strike capability. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265; @mon1tor_ua/67271)
Russian air‑defence reported downing 127 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions in a single night, reflecting intense air‑defence engagements. (Source: @tass_agency/378193)
Finland announced its 33rd military aid package to Ukraine valued at €128 million, adding to prior deliveries of €3.4 billion. (Source: @UkraineNow/81542)
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were issued for south‑east, east and north‑east directions, highlighting increased aerial threat to near‑front areas. (Source: @kpszsu/63295)
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are making incremental territorial gains in the Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, but face intensified Russian aerial bombardment across multiple fronts. The proliferation of UAVs and missile strikes, especially on logistical hubs like Kremenchuk, underscores a sustained Russian effort to disrupt supply lines and command structures. Russian strategic bomber movements and air‑defence alerts suggest preparation for larger‑scale operations, while Ukrainian air‑defence continues to engage heavily, indicating a contested airspace.
sections.regional_response: International partners remain supportive, exemplified by Finland's €128 million aid package, while NATO allies continue to provide air‑defence assets that have downed numerous drones. Russian diplomatic activity, including statements on UAV incidents in neighboring countries, reflects an attempt to manage the broader geopolitical fallout. Ukrainian leadership urges civilian vigilance amid heightened alert levels, particularly in Kyiv and other near‑front regions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths over Chernihiv and Zaporizhzhia is essential for early warning of potential strike vectors. The recent relocation of Tu‑95MS bombers to Engels may indicate a shift in long‑range strike posture. Russian air‑defence claims of high UAV kill counts should be cross‑checked with independent observations to assess actual UAV operational tempo. The pattern of missile attacks on mid‑size cities suggests a focus on disrupting regional logistics and morale.
